task.title: Auth guard on /ingest/2d + rate limit annotation
task.why: POST /api/v1/ingest/2d has no authentication. Any internet client can push arbitrary data into the catalog pipeline. Must require X-Admin-API-Key when ADMIN_API_KEY is configured.

implementation.step:
# At top of file, add import: from app.core.security import require_admin_api_key from fastapi import Depends
# Find the ingest_2d route and add Depends: # BEFORE: @router.post("/2d", response_model=DrawingIngestResponse) def ingest_2d(request: DrawingIngestRequest, db: Session = Depends(get_db)):
# AFTER: @router.post("/2d", response_model=DrawingIngestResponse) def ingest_2d( request: DrawingIngestRequest, db: Session = Depends(get_db), _auth: None = Depends(require_admin_api_key), ):
Note: require_admin_api_key already exists in core/security.py and returns None when admin_api_key is not configured (open access in local dev). The Depends() pattern matches the existing vendor-assets route.

tests.code:
from fastapi.testclient import TestClient from app.main import app from app.core.config import settings
client = TestClient(app)
def test_ingest_2d_requires_admin_key_when_configured(): settings.admin_api_key = "test-admin-secret" try: r = client.post( "/api/v1/ingest/2d", json={"product_id": "hex-bolt-iso4014", "content": "M8 d:8.0 L:30"}, ) assert r.status_code == 401 finally: settings.admin_api_key = None
def test_ingest_2d_accepts_valid_admin_key(): settings.admin_api_key = "test-admin-secret" try: r = client.post( "/api/v1/ingest/2d", headers={"X-Admin-API-Key": "test-admin-secret"}, json={"product_id": "hex-bolt-iso4014", "content": "M8 d:8.0 L:30"}, ) assert r.status_code in (200, 422) # 422 = valid auth, bad body finally: settings.admin_api_key = None
def test_ingest_2d_open_when_no_admin_key_configured(): settings.admin_api_key = None r = client.post( "/api/v1/ingest/2d", json={"product_id": "hex-bolt-iso4014", "content": "M8 d:8.0 L:30"}, ) assert r.status_code != 401

task.commit_message: fix(T04): require admin API key on /ingest/2d endpoint

task.title: Nginx config — TLS, rate limiting, immutable GLB caching
task.why: No reverse proxy → API port 8000 exposed directly, no rate limiting, no TLS, no gzip. Geometry hash URLs can't be CDN-cached. Nginx must sit in front of API and enforce limits per route.
task.commit_message: feat(T05): Nginx config — TLS, rate limiting, immutable GLB headers

task.title: Docker Compose — production-safe env, healthchecks, volumes
task.why: docker-compose.yml uses env_file: .env.example (placeholder passwords). No healthchecks on Redis or Postgres — API can start before DB is ready. No persistent volumes for Postgres or MinIO — data lost on container restart. API port 8000 exposed directly — should only be behind Nginx.

implementation.step:
FULL REPLACEMENT:
# mecsu.vn Fastener CAD Platform — Docker Compose # Usage: # cp infra/.env.production.example infra/.env # # Edit infra/.env — change ALL CHANGE_ME values # cd infra && docker compose up -d # # NEVER commit infra/.env
version: "3.9"
x-api-common: &api-common build: context: .. dockerfile: infra/Dockerfile.api env_file: .env volumes: - ../backend/storage:/app/storage restart: unless-stopped depends_on: postgres: condition: service_healthy redis: condition: service_healthy
services:
# ── API (2 replicas possible: docker compose scale api=2) ────────── api: <<: *api-common command: uvicorn app.main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --workers 2 # Do NOT expose 8000 publicly — only Nginx reaches it expose: - "8000" environment: MODEL_SYNC_GENERATION: "false" healthcheck: test: ["CMD", "python", "-c", "import urllib.request; urllib.request.urlopen('http://localhost:8000/health')"] interval: 15s timeout: 5s retries: 3 start_period: 30s
# ── Celery workers ───────────────────────────────────────────────── worker: <<: *api-common command: > celery -A app.workers.tasks:celery_app worker -Q preview_fast,cad_generate,engineering_step,batch_pregenerate --loglevel=info --concurrency=2 environment: MODEL_SYNC_GENERATION: "false" deploy: resources: limits: memory: 2G # CadQuery/OCC is memory-hungry healthcheck: test: ["CMD", "celery", "-A", "app.workers.tasks:celery_app", "inspect", "ping", "-d", "celery@$$HOSTNAME"] interval: 30s timeout: 10s retries: 3 start_period: 60s
# ── Nginx (production) ───────────────────────────────────────────── nginx: image: nginx:1.27-alpine restart: unless-stopped ports: - "80:80" - "443:443" volumes: - ./nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf:ro - ./ssl:/etc/nginx/ssl:ro depends_on: api: condition: service_healthy
# ── Redis 7 ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────── redis: image: redis:7-alpine restart: unless-stopped command: > redis-server --requirepass ${REDIS_PASSWORD} --maxmemory 512mb --maxmemory-policy allkeys-lru --save 900 1 --save 300 10 volumes: - redis_data:/data expose: - "6379" healthcheck: test: ["CMD", "redis-cli", "--no-auth-warning", "-a", "${REDIS_PASSWORD}", "ping"] interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5
# ── PostgreSQL 16 ───────────────────────────────────────────────── postgres: image: postgres:16-alpine restart: unless-stopped environment: POSTGRES_DB: ${POSTGRES_DB} POSTGRES_USER: ${POSTGRES_USER} POSTGRES_PASSWORD: ${POSTGRES_PASSWORD} volumes: - postgres_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data expose: - "5432" healthcheck: test: ["CMD-SHELL", "pg_isready -U ${POSTGRES_USER} -d ${POSTGRES_DB}"] interval: 10s timeout: 5s retries: 5
# ── MinIO (S3-compatible local storage) ─────────────────────────── minio: image: minio/minio:RELEASE.2025-04-22T22-12-26Z restart: unless-stopped command: server /data --console-address ":9001" environment: MINIO_ROOT_USER: ${MINIO_ROOT_USER} MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD: ${MINIO_ROOT_PASSWORD} volumes: - minio_data:/data expose: - "9000" ports: - "9001:9001" # MinIO Console — restrict in production firewall healthcheck: test: ["CMD", "mc", "ready", "local"] interval: 30s timeout: 10s retries: 3 start_period: 15s
volumes: postgres_data: driver: local redis_data: driver: local minio_data: driver: local

task.commit_message: fix(T06): docker-compose production-safe — .env, healthchecks, persistent volumes, no exposed API port
